title: Autonomous Software Development with MetaGPT

modules.title: Multi-Agent Software Engineering: What MetaGPT Does and Where It Fits
modules.sub: Mapping MetaGPT's capabilities against real engineering team needs
modules.description: Learners survey how MetaGPT coordinates multiple specialized agents to simulate a software development team, and assess what it can realistically produce versus what it cannot. They position MetaGPT within the broader landscape of AI-assisted development tools and evaluate where it offers genuine leverage for their organization.

modules.title: Role Architecture: Product Manager, Architect, Engineer, and QA Agents
modules.sub: Understanding how each agent role contributes to software output
modules.description: Learners examine the discrete responsibilities assigned to each agent role inside MetaGPT and how those roles hand off artifacts to one another. They analyze how role boundaries affect output quality and identify which agent behaviors require the closest human scrutiny.

modules.title: Translating Requirements into Agent Workflows: From Brief to Code
modules.sub: Crafting inputs that produce structured, useful agent-driven outputs
modules.description: Learners practice writing and refining natural language requirements briefs that MetaGPT can interpret and execute with minimal drift. They trace the transformation from initial brief through design artifacts to generated code, identifying where ambiguity in the input propagates into flawed outputs downstream.

modules.title: Reviewing, Correcting, and Auditing AI-Generated Codebases
modules.sub: Applying rigorous human review to code you did not write yourself
modules.description: Learners develop a structured approach to auditing MetaGPT-generated code for correctness, security vulnerabilities, and maintainability. They practice identifying common failure patterns in generated code and apply correction strategies that preserve useful output while eliminating unsafe or incorrect logic.

modules.title: Integrating MetaGPT into Existing Development Pipelines and CI/CD
modules.sub: Connecting autonomous code generation to your real-world toolchain
modules.description: Learners design integration points between MetaGPT workflows and existing version control, code review, and CI/CD infrastructure. They evaluate the practical tradeoffs of different integration depths and define the gatekeeping steps required before AI-generated artifacts can proceed toward production.

modules.title: Failure Modes, Hallucinated APIs, and When to Override the Agents
modules.sub: Recognizing when MetaGPT output is confidently wrong and acting decisively
modules.description: Learners catalog the characteristic failure modes of MetaGPT, including fabricated library calls, misinterpreted requirements, and architectural decisions that are internally consistent but externally broken. They build decision criteria for when to correct, regenerate, or abandon agent output entirely and escalate to human-only development.

modules.title: Governance and Accountability in Autonomous Code Generation
modules.sub: Defining who owns decisions when agents write production software
modules.description: Learners construct a governance framework that assigns clear accountability for AI-generated code across engineering, legal, and security stakeholders. They establish policy boundaries covering acceptable use cases, mandatory review thresholds, audit trails, and conditions under which autonomous generation must be suspended.

modules.title: Running a Real Project: End-to-End with MetaGPT in Your Work Context
modules.sub: Applying the full course workflow to a project from your own environment
modules.description: Learners execute a complete MetaGPT-assisted development cycle on a scoped project relevant to their actual work context, from requirements brief through code review and integration checkpoints. They document the decisions made at each governance boundary and produce a reflective assessment of where the tool added value and where human judgment proved indispensable.

synopsis: MetaGPT operationalizes a full software development team as a coordinated multi-agent system — assigning roles, producing structured artifacts, and generating working code from natural language requirements. This course is designed for senior developers, engineering managers, and technical leads who want to understand what autonomous software development actually delivers today, how to integrate it responsibly, and where human oversight remains non-negotiable.
rationale: Multi-agent code generation tools are moving from research demonstrations to real development pipelines, and technical leaders who cannot critically evaluate their outputs and failure modes risk shipping AI-generated code without adequate oversight.
learning_outcome: Learners will be able to configure and run a MetaGPT-assisted development workflow, critically audit its outputs for correctness and security, and define the governance boundaries required for responsible use in a production engineering environment.
source_signals: OpenCode – Open source AI coding agent (score: 3131)
Open source AI is the path forward (score: 5021)
